subject: ap-government
title: Quantitative Analysis — Women Candidates for Senate by Party, 1980–2020
stimulus: Sources: Colorado Public Radio, 2018; Ballotpedia, 2022. Line graph showing Women Candidates for Senate by Party from 1980 to 2020 for Democratic and Republican women candidates in general elections.

parts.prompt: Identify the year with the fewest number of Democratic women candidates for the Senate.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Identifies 1988 as the year with the fewest number of Democratic women candidates for the Senate.

parts.prompt: Describe the difference in an overall trend in Democratic and Republican women candidates for the Senate as illustrated in the graph.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Describes the difference in trends. Accept: there is an overall increase in female Senate candidates for both Democrats and Republicans, but the increase for Democrats is larger than for Republicans; there are more female Democratic candidates than female Republican candidates in every election since 1996.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must describe both trends and note the difference between them — not just describe one party's trend. Accept any accurate description of the difference.

parts.prompt: Draw a conclusion that explains an overall trend shown in the graph.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Draws a conclusion explaining a trend. Accept: the total number of female Senate candidates increased over time because both parties are responding to voters who want to vote for women; a broader recognition of the role of women in society has led to more women choosing to run for the Senate; Democrats have been more successful in recruiting female Senate candidates than Republicans.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must go beyond describing the trend to drawing a conclusion about why it exists. Accept any plausible conclusion supported by the data.

parts.prompt: Explain how the data in the graph might affect political participation.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Explains how the data might affect political participation. Accept: women may be more encouraged to vote when they see female candidates on the ballot; since there are more women candidates than before, political participation might increase because different demographic groups will feel more empowered by the political process; participation may decline among individuals who prefer male candidates.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must explain a specific connection between the data about women candidates and political participation. Accept any plausible explanation.
